_metadata.topic: rc.19 bootstrap consolidation — snapshot + propagation + drift + marker migration test coverage

test_runner.name: vitest
test_runner.notes: Every package uses the same vitest 3.2.4. All test files follow the vitest API: describe/it/expect/vi/beforeEach/afterEach. No jest, no mocha.

relevant_files.rationale: Already contains a full managed-block test block (lines 442–574) covering the EXACT same surface rc.19 wants to migrate: SECTION_BEGIN/SECTION_END markers, countOccurrences helper, single-pair assertion, idempotency, in-place replace, and user-vandalism overwrite. The marker strings are literal 'fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end' — rc.19 needs to migrate these to 'fabric:bootstrap:begin/end' and these tests are the canonical regression baseline.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Test-patterns exploration MUST mirror this file as the primary template for the three-end propagation test and the marker migration test. Every rc.19 propagation/drift/migration test should adopt this file's structure: countOccurrences helper, marker-bracketing assertions (beginIdx < headingIdx < endIdx), and the four-axis matrix (presence × idempotency × in-place-replace × overwrite).

key_code.description: Module-level constants holding the literal marker strings — rc.19 will need a NEW pair ('fabric:bootstrap:begin/end') plus a LEGACY pair ('fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end') for the migration test. Pattern: declare both at module scope and use them in test fixtures + assertions.

Helper that counts non-overlapping substring occurrences. Used to assert 'exactly one marker pair after re-install' which is the core idempotency contract for managed blocks. rc.19 marker migration test will use this to assert: 0 legacy markers + 1 new marker pair after migration.

key_code.symbol: Test 'writes one begin/end marker pair (with heading between) on first install across all three targets'
key_code.description: Seeds AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor/rules with user content, runs init, asserts: exactly one begin marker + one end marker, heading present between, beginIdx < headingIdx < endIdx (ordering), pre-existing user content preserved verbatim via content.startsWith(seedContent). This is the EXACT shape rc.19's three-end propagation test should take.

key_code.symbol: Test 'is idempotent: re-running install yields byte-identical files'
key_code.description: Snapshots file content after first runInit, runs again, asserts byte-equality + still exactly one marker pair. rc.19 snapshot write test (writing .fabric/AGENTS.md) should mirror this.

key_code.symbol: Test 'replaces the section in place when fabric_language changes (no duplication, no orphan section)'
key_code.description: Mutates .fabric/fabric-config.json (flips fabric_language), re-runs init, asserts old content gone + new content present + still exactly one marker pair. rc.19 marker migration test should mirror this: seed file with LEGACY marker, run install (or doctor --fix), assert legacy marker gone + new marker present + exactly one pair.

key_code.symbol: Test 'overwrites user edits inside the markers on re-install (managed-section convention)'
key_code.description: Injects 'USER VANDALISM' string inside markers, re-runs init, asserts vandalism wiped + canonical content restored + pre-section user content preserved. rc.19 drift detection test (--fix overwrite) should mirror this assertion shape.

relevant_files.rationale: rc.17 regression test explicitly called out in the task brief. Demonstrates the cleanest pattern for: (1) tmpdir-based isolation via mkdtempSync + tempRoots[] + afterEach cleanup, (2) writing a fixture file to disk, (3) calling a writer/installer directly, (4) re-reading and asserting on the byte content, (5) idempotency via two writes + byte-equality, (6) legacy→new migration in a single test. The four-test matrix here (presence / preservation / idempotency / legacy migration) is exactly the shape rc.19 snapshot+migration tests need.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Mirror this file's structure verbatim for rc.19's marker-migration test: fixture with old marker → run --fix → assert new marker AND no duplicate AND surrounding content preserved AND idempotent on second --fix. The 'occurrences = written.match(/.../g) ?? []; expect(occurrences).toHaveLength(1)' pattern (L142-143, L169-170) is the cleanest assertion idiom for 'exactly one marker pair' and should replace countOccurrences where the project moves toward regex.

key_code.description: Idiomatic tmpdir pattern: mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'prefix-')), push root to module-level array, afterEach pops and rmSync(root, {recursive:true, force:true}). Every rc.19 integration test that doesn't need the cocos fixture should use this pattern.

Fixture content + assertions list co-located at module scope. The fixture has a one-line comment explaining its origin and a structured comment listing what's covered. rc.19 marker migration fixture should adopt this commenting style — a legacy-marker fixture with explicit 'this is what 'fab install' wrote in rc.18' comment.

Seed legacy fixture, run writer, assert: legacy spelling gone + new spelling present + original sections intact + exactly one occurrence via regex match. This is the EXACT template for rc.19 marker-migration test ('fabric:knowledge-base:*' → 'fabric:bootstrap:*'). Direct copy candidate.

key_code.symbol: Test 'is idempotent — invoking write() twice yields byte-equal output'
key_code.description: Two-write byte-equality + 'present once' check. rc.19 snapshot test should mirror: two `fab install` invocations on a clean fixture, assert .fabric/AGENTS.md byte-equal across runs.

relevant_files.rationale: Direct precedent for the rc.19 drift detection (byte-level) test. Scenarios 2 and 3 (file head comment L1-L27) cover exactly: 'missing file auto-applies' (the --fix overwrite case) and 'drift aborts with a helpful message' (the report-only abort case). Uses seedDriftedFile() and seedMissingFile() helpers from init-test-utils.ts which mutate managed files byte-wise — the exact mechanism for simulating drift in rc.19's two-layer drift detection.
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 drift detection test must mirror this file's: (1) byte-mutate via seedDriftedFile to simulate user-edit drift, (2) assert thrownError with regex matching 'fab doctor' + 'fab uninstall'/'--fix', (3) separate test for missing-file auto-restore. Also see captureStdio() L51-L75 for the stdio-capture pattern needed when asserting on doctor's drift report output.

key_code.description: Spies on process.stderr.write AND console.log, collects into arrays, returns restore() callback. rc.19 drift test needs this to assert the drift-abort message format (which goes to stderr) without polluting test output.

snapshotTree before/after, assert equality on managed dirs (.claude/.codex/.cursor/.fabric), assert specific i18n message in stdio capture. rc.19 drift detection 'no drift detected' happy-path test mirrors this exactly. Note the events.jsonl exception comment (L106-L108) — diff-mode appends install_diff_applied, so events.jsonl is excluded from byte-equality. rc.19 must adopt the same exception.

key_code.symbol: Scenario 3 reference — 'drift aborts with a helpful message'
key_code.description: Pattern: seedDriftedFile mutates managed file → expect(runInit(target)).rejects + assert error.message matches /fab doctor/ AND /fab uninstall/. rc.19 doctor drift test should: (1) seed drift via seedDriftedFile on .fabric/AGENTS.md or .claude/CLAUDE.md, (2) assert doctor without --fix reports drift + abort exit, (3) assert doctor --fix overwrites and re-canonicalizes.

relevant_files.rationale: Contains the cleanest 'thrown error with semantic message' assertion pattern (L72-L80): wrap async call in try/catch into a thrownError variable, assert non-null + assert message matches multiple regex. rc.19 drift abort test needs this exact pattern. Also demonstrates seedDriftedFile usage for byte-level drift simulation on .fabric/agents.meta.json (L65).
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 drift abort assertion approach: copy the L70-L80 idiom verbatim. The error message contract is locked: must mention conflicting path + 'fab doctor' + 'fab uninstall'. rc.19 doctor drift abort test must verify the same three substrings, with the added requirement that the byte-level mismatch report appears in stderr.

key_code.symbol: Test 'aborts with a drift message when a managed hook script has been byte-modified'
key_code.description: Canonical drift-abort assertion shape: runInit OK → seedDriftedFile(target, '.fabric/agents.meta.json', () => '{}\n') → try { await runInit(target); } catch (e) { thrownError = e; } → expect(thrownError).not.toBeNull() → expect(thrownError!.message).toMatch(/.../) for each required substring. Direct copy-paste candidate for rc.19 drift tests.

key_code.symbol: Test 'existing legacy v1.x bootstrap files are untouched by v2.0 init'
key_code.description: Pre-seeds a legacy file path before init, asserts content unchanged after init. rc.19 'Codex CLI nested AGENTS.md discovery behavior' (if testable) could mirror this — pre-seed nested AGENTS.md, run install, assert nested file untouched.
Tiny createHash('sha256').update(content).digest('hex') helper for content-hash comparisons. rc.19 idempotency tests across runs can use this instead of full-content comparison for terser assertions.

relevant_files.rationale: Single source of truth for fixture creation, runInit invocation, snapshotTree, and drift seeding. Every rc.19 integration test will import from this file. The runInit() at L99-L112 drives `fab install` end-to-end via buildInitExecutionPlan + executeInitExecutionPlan with skipMcp:true — this is the canonical way to invoke install in tests and rc.19 propagation tests must use it.
relevant_files.topic_relation: All rc.19 integration tests should import from this file. New helpers may be needed: seedLegacyMarker(root, relPath) to write a file with old 'fabric:knowledge-base:*' markers; assertExactlyOneMarkerPair(content, beginMarker, endMarker) — but check existing patterns first to keep helper count low.

key_code.description: Copies fixtures/cocos-stub to a unique tmpdir path (includes pid, Date.now, random suffix → safe under parallel test runners). Removes pre-existing AGENTS.md / .fabric/ / .claude/ so tests start from a 'has-source-code-but-no-fabric-state' baseline. Every rc.19 integration test uses this to set up a project root.
mkdirSync(dirname, recursive:true) + writeFileSync(utf8). rc.19 marker-migration tests use this to seed a legacy AGENTS.md with old markers before running install.

Calls buildInitExecutionPlan({target, options:{skipMcp:true, planOnly:opts.planOnly}, interactive:false}) then executeInitExecutionPlan. skipMcp:true is mandatory in fixture tests (MCP install writes outside the sandbox). rc.19 tests must use this — not direct calls to initFabric — to exercise the full bootstrap stage.

Recursively walks a subtree and returns {relativePath: utf8Content} map. Used for byte-identical idempotency assertions across re-runs. rc.19 three-end propagation test snapshots .claude/.codex/.cursor and asserts equality across two runInit calls.
Read managed file → apply modifier callback → write back. The byte-level drift simulator. rc.19 doctor drift test calls this on .fabric/AGENTS.md to trigger the new two-layer drift detector.
rmSync(target, {force:true}). Simulates the 'user deleted a managed file' case which doctor --fix should auto-restore. rc.19 doctor --fix test uses this for the missing-file recovery scenario.

relevant_files.rationale: rc.16 F2-tests reference — demonstrates the loading pattern for .cjs templates via createRequire + absolute path under templates/, and the matrix-style coverage shape (11 keys × 4 variants). If rc.19 introduces template-side helpers (e.g. a managed-block writer .cjs), use this loading idiom.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Less critical for rc.19 (rc.19 is install-side TS, not template-side cjs), but the matrix coverage shape (assert every key × every variant) is worth borrowing for the three-end propagation test: assert every marker × every client (.claude/.codex/.cursor) × every state (present/idempotent/drift). The createRequire pattern at L35-L38 is also useful if rc.19 tests need to load templates from disk.

key_code.description: const require = createRequire(import.meta.url); const libPath = fileURLToPath(new URL('../templates/hooks/lib/banner-i18n.cjs', import.meta.url)); const lib = require(libPath) as LibModule. Standard idiom for loading shipped templates from tests.

relevant_files.rationale: Demonstrates the mocked-server doctor invocation pattern (vi.doMock '@fenglimg/fabric-server' + import doctorCommand dynamically). rc.19 doctor --fix tests at the CLI level should mirror this. Also shows the captureStdout helper (L633-L643) for asserting on doctor's JSON/text output. The --fix path test (L73-L106) is the closest precedent for rc.19's drift --fix surface.
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 has TWO layers of drift-detection tests: (a) byte-level drift surface at the install-diff layer (mirror install-diff-mode.test.ts), and (b) doctor-level CLI flag behavior (mirror this file). For the CLI layer test, copy the vi.doMock pattern and assert: doctor without --fix → status=error + exit 1 + drift report; doctor --fix → status=ok + repaired summary + exit 0.

key_code.description: vi.spyOn(process.stdout, 'write').mockImplementation(...) into a lines[] array + restore() callback. Reused across all doctor CLI tests.

Mock the server module BEFORE await import('../src/commands/doctor.ts'), then call doctorCommand.run({args:{...}}). Required because doctor.ts imports from fabric-server at module load. rc.19 doctor drift-overwrite tests must follow this pattern to inject mocked runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix returns.

key_code.symbol: afterEach cleanup: vi.restoreAllMocks + vi.resetModules + vi.doUnmock + restore process.exitCode
key_code.description: Critical for dynamic-mock tests — without resetModules() the second test's vi.doMock won't take effect on re-import. rc.19 doctor CLI tests must include this exact teardown.

Returns a fully-typed DoctorReport shape so individual tests just override status / arrays. rc.19 doctor drift CLI tests should extend this with a 'drift' status variant carrying drifted_files: [...] payload.

relevant_files.rationale: Server-side doctor tests at the service layer (runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix called directly, not via CLI). Shows the FABRIC_HOME tmpdir-isolation pattern (L20-L40) — every doctor test isolates ~/.fabric to a per-test tmpdir to prevent the developer's real personal knowledge dir from leaking into assertions. rc.19 doctor tests touching personal scope MUST adopt this isolation.
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 doctor drift detection at the SERVICE layer (the actual byte-comparison logic) should be tested here, not at the CLI layer. Mirror the createProject/createInitializedProject pattern + per-test FABRIC_HOME isolation. The L42-L69 test 'reports target-state fixable and manual errors' shows the assertion shape: report.fixable_errors.map(i => i.code) array equality. rc.19 adds a new error code (managed_block_drift or bootstrap_snapshot_drift) — append it to this array assertion.

key_code.description: originalFabricHome = process.env.FABRIC_HOME; const fakeHome = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'doctor-fabric-home-')); tempRoots.push(fakeHome); process.env.FABRIC_HOME = fakeHome. Without this, doctor's personal-knowledge scan walks the developer's real ~/.fabric and pollutes the report. MANDATORY for rc.19 doctor service tests.

Asserts the exact array of error codes returned by runDoctorReport. rc.19's new drift codes (e.g. 'managed_block_drift', 'snapshot_drift') will need entries here when rc.19 service-layer doctor tests are added.

relevant_files.rationale: Task brief asked whether this is a relevant precedent for the install snapshot concept. Answer: NO — this test exercises forensic ASSERTIONS (framework detection emitting evidence-backed claims about the project), not the install-time snapshot mechanism. The 'shadow' here refers to forensic shadow-mirroring (the scanner's mirrored evidence model), unrelated to the rc.19 bootstrap snapshot at .fabric/AGENTS.md. Skip this as a pattern source.
relevant_files.topic_relation: Anti-pattern signal: do NOT model rc.19 snapshot tests on forensic-shadow-mirroring. The forensic test uses toMatchInlineSnapshot for complex object equality, which would be the wrong tool for rc.19 (we need byte-level file content assertions, not object snapshots).

relevant_files.rationale: The shared 'werewolf' fixture used by createWerewolfFixtureRoot — a stub Cocos Creator project (AGENTS.md, assets/, package.json, project.config.json, README.md, tsconfig.json). Every rc.19 integration test inherits this baseline. The pre-existing AGENTS.md is auto-deleted by createWerewolfFixtureRoot (L26-L28) so tests get a clean state.
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 marker-migration tests need to either (a) start from this fixture and write a legacy-marker AGENTS.md as a setup step via writeFixtureFile, or (b) consider whether a new fixture variant (e.g. 'legacy-rc18-stub') is warranted. Recommend (a) — keep the fixture surface small, materialize legacy state in test setup.

key_code.description: AGENTS.md (deleted on copy), assets/, package.json, project.config.json, README.md, tsconfig.json. Minimal Cocos Creator stub — enough for the forensic scanner to detect framework=cocos-creator, which drives realistic install behavior.

relevant_files.rationale: Symmetric counterpart to install-skills-and-hooks — verifies uninstall removes the managed block cleanly and preserves user content outside markers. Relevant for rc.19 because the marker migration may need to assert that 'uninstall' on a legacy-marker file still works (cleanup symmetry).
relevant_files.topic_relation: rc.19 marker-migration ALSO needs to test the uninstall path: after migration to new markers, `fab uninstall` must remove the new markers cleanly. Recommend adding one parallel test in this file or alongside the migration test.

convention_a.name: fixture-based (most install/doctor integration tests)
convention_a.isolation: cpSync(WEREWOLF_FIXTURE, root, {recursive:true}) + per-test rm of AGENTS.md / .fabric / .claude
convention_a.use_when: Test needs a realistic project root with source files (Cocos stub) so the forensic scanner produces real assertions and install touches realistic state.

convention_b.name: raw mkdtempSync (writer/parser unit-ish tests)
convention_b.isolation: empty tmpdir, test seeds exactly the files needed via writeFileSync
convention_b.use_when: Test exercises a single writer/parser in isolation (e.g. TOML writer, banner-i18n loader) where a full project root would be overhead.

tmpdir_conventions.rc19_recommendation: Use convention A (createWerewolfFixtureRoot) for: snapshot test, three-end propagation test, marker migration test, doctor drift test. Use convention B (raw mkdtempSync) only if rc.19 introduces a new unit-level managed-block writer that warrants direct testing without a full install plan.

shared_fixture.purpose: Stub Cocos Creator project — drives realistic forensic detection without a full real game project
shared_fixture.state_at_copy: AGENTS.md, assets/, package.json, project.config.json, README.md, tsconfig.json (pre-existing AGENTS.md gets deleted by createWerewolfFixtureRoot to give a clean baseline)

fixture_conventions.per_test_setup: Use writeFixtureFile(root, relPath, content) to materialize fixture-specific state (e.g. legacy markers, drifted files) AFTER createWerewolfFixtureRoot returns. Don't add new top-level fixtures unless multiple tests need the same legacy state.
fixture_conventions.rc19_recommendation: Do NOT introduce a 'legacy-rc18-stub' fixture. Instead: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → runInit (gets canonical rc.19 state) → seedDriftedFile/writeFixtureFile to roll specific bytes back to a legacy shape. This keeps fixture maintenance to one location.

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.rc19_task: Snapshot write test — `fab install` writes .fabric/AGENTS.md with current canonical content
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → runInit → readFileSync('.fabric/AGENTS.md') → assert (a) file exists, (b) contains expected canonical markers/headings, (c) is byte-equal to a baseline template (if a template ships) OR matches structural assertions (begin/heading/end ordering, exactly one marker pair). Then second runInit → assert byte-identical (idempotency).
assertions: existsSync('.fabric/AGENTS.md')
content contains '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->' (exactly once via .match regex)
content contains '<!-- fabric:bootstrap:end -->' (exactly once)
beginIdx < headingIdx < endIdx ordering
second runInit produces byte-identical .fabric/AGENTS.md (snapshot1 === snapshot2)

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.rc19_task: Three-end propagation test — `fab install` writes thin-shell Claude CLAUDE.md + Codex AGENTS.md managed block + Cursor .mdc managed block
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → seed pre-existing AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor/rules with user content via writeFixtureFile → runInit → loop over the three target paths and assert: exactly one new-marker pair, heading bracketed, pre-existing user content preserved verbatim above the section. Second runInit → snapshotTree equality on .claude/.codex/.cursor.
assertions: for each of ['.claude/CLAUDE.md' or thin-shell path, 'AGENTS.md' or Codex-target, '.cursor/rules.mdc']: countOccurrences(content, NEW_BEGIN) === 1 && countOccurrences(content, NEW_END) === 1
Each managed block contains the expected snapshot-reference content (e.g. '@.fabric/AGENTS.md' include directive — depends on rc.19 design)
snapshot2 === snapshot1 for .claude, .codex, .cursor trees
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.helpers_needed: createWerewolfFixtureRoot, runInit, snapshotTree, writeFixtureFile, countOccurrences (lift to helpers if reused across new tests)

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.rc19_task: Doctor two-layer drift detection — byte-level; abort without --fix; --fix overwrites
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.primary_pattern: Hybrid — packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-diff-mode.test.ts (byte-mutate + abort assertion) + packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts (--fix CLI surface)
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: TWO test files / blocks: (1) SERVICE-LEVEL in packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts — runDoctorReport on a drift-seeded fixture returns status=error with new code (e.g. 'managed_block_drift') in fixable_errors[]; runDoctorFix re-applies canonical content. (2) CLI-LEVEL in packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts — vi.doMock runDoctorReport to return the new drift code, assert exit code 1 + drift section in stdout; vi.doMock runDoctorFix.changed=true, assert exit 0 + fix-summary line.
assertions: Service: report.fixable_errors.map(i => i.code) contains 'managed_block_drift'
Service: after runDoctorFix, content of drifted file is byte-equal to canonical template
Service: byte-comparison is the detection trigger (not structural) — mutate even ONE whitespace char and drift fires
CLI: doctor without --fix → process.exitCode === 1 + stderr/stdout mentions 'fab doctor --fix'
CLI: doctor --fix → process.exitCode === 0 (or originalExitCode) + 'Applied N deterministic doctor fix(es)' line
CLI: doctor --fix is idempotent — second run reports zero changes
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.helpers_needed: createWerewolfFixtureRoot, runInit, seedDriftedFile (existing); captureStdout from doctor.test.ts; createReport factory extended with drift status; vi.doMock pattern from doctor.test.ts L18-L43

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → runInit (gets canonical rc.18 state with OLD markers — OR materialize OLD markers via writeFixtureFile to simulate an rc.18 install) → run `fab doctor --fix` (or migration-triggering install path, depending on rc.19 design) → assert: OLD markers gone, NEW markers present, exactly one NEW pair, surrounding user content preserved, second --fix is a no-op (idempotency).
assertions: content.match(/<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:(begin|end) -->/g) === null (legacy gone)
content.match(/<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->/g).length === 1
content.match(/<!-- fabric:bootstrap:end -->/g).length === 1
Pre-section and post-section user content survives byte-identical
Second migration run produces byte-identical file (idempotent)
Inside the new markers: heading present, ordering beginIdx < headingIdx < endIdx
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.helpers_needed: createWerewolfFixtureRoot, writeFixtureFile (materialize legacy state directly OR via a small seedLegacyMarkers helper if reused). Consider extending init-test-utils.ts with a seedLegacyBootstrapMarkers(root) helper that writes the three-end legacy fixture in one call.

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.rc19_task: Codex CLI nested AGENTS.md discovery — what to tell users
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.primary_pattern: Documentation, NOT test (or thin doc-level assertion test)
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: RECOMMENDATION: do NOT write a runtime test for this. Codex's discovery is a black-box runtime behavior of the Codex CLI binary — we can't simulate it in vitest without spawning Codex. Instead: (a) add a documentation assertion test that grep's the rc.19 docs for the canonical nested-AGENTS.md guidance string (lightweight regression on the docs themselves), OR (b) skip testing entirely and treat as a docs-only deliverable.
assertions: (if test added) readFileSync('docs/codex-nested-agents.md').includes('canonical guidance string')

rc19_task_to_pattern_map.primary_pattern: packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L512-L539 ('replaces the section in place when fabric_language changes')
rc19_task_to_pattern_map.test_shape: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → writeFixtureFile('.fabric/project-rules.md', 'user-authored project rules\n') → runInit → assert .fabric/AGENTS.md (or downstream propagation targets) include the project-rules content concatenated at the documented location → re-run with .fabric/project-rules.md ABSENT → assert content gracefully omitted, no error, idempotent re-runs.
assertions: When project-rules.md present: snapshot content contains project-rules verbatim
When project-rules.md absent: snapshot content is structurally valid (no broken includes, no orphan headings)
Re-runs with/without project-rules.md are each independently idempotent
Changing project-rules.md content → next install propagates the new content (managed-block replace-in-place)

drift_test_approach.detection_layer: byte-level — strict equality between on-disk content and the rc.19-canonical template content
drift_test_approach.implementation_pattern: Mirror packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts existing check pattern: read on-disk file → compute against canonical (either a packaged template or the regenerated managed-block content) → if !==, push to fixable_errors[] with new code (suggested: 'managed_block_drift' or 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift' — pick one and document).

test_shape.happy_path: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → runInit → runDoctorReport(target) → expect report.status === 'ok' AND no 'managed_block_drift' code in fixable_errors[]
test_shape.drift_path: createWerewolfFixtureRoot → runInit → seedDriftedFile(target, '.fabric/AGENTS.md', orig => orig.replace('## Some Heading', '## TAMPERED Heading')) → runDoctorReport → expect status === 'error' + fixable_errors[].some(e => e.code === 'managed_block_drift')
test_shape.fix_path: Continue from drift_path → await runDoctorFix(target) → expect result.changed === true + result.fixed[].some(f => f.code === 'managed_block_drift') → re-run runDoctorReport → expect status === 'ok' (drift gone)
test_shape.abort_path_cli: vi.doMock runDoctorReport to return drift status → await doctorCommand.run({args:{target, fix:false}}) → expect process.exitCode === 1 + stdout includes 'fab doctor --fix' suggestion

byte_equality_caveats: events.jsonl is line-appended on every install; exclude from byte-equality assertions (see install-diff-mode.test.ts L106-L108 precedent)
Timestamps in agents.meta.json may force content-shape rather than byte equality — check the existing meta-stable patterns
CRLF risk on Windows — see test_concerns.crlf_handling

marker_migration_test_approach.fixture_seeding: Materialize the legacy state via writeFixtureFile, NOT via a new top-level fixture. Seed AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor/rules each with: pre-section user content + '<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin -->\n<canonical-rc18-content>\n<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:end -->' + post-section user content.
marker_migration_test_approach.migration_trigger: Open question for rc.19 design: is migration triggered by `fab install` (silent in-place rewrite) OR by `fab doctor --fix` (after a 'legacy_marker_detected' fixable_error)? Recommend the latter for traceability — every migration appears in the doctor report.
assertions_per_target: Legacy markers absent: content.match(/<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:(begin|end) -->/g) === null
New markers present exactly once each: content.match(/<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin -->/g).length === 1
Idempotency: re-running migration on already-migrated file produces byte-identical output
edge_cases_worth_testing: User content INSIDE the legacy markers (managed-section convention says this gets overwritten — assert it does, matches install-skills-and-hooks L541-L573 precedent)
Legacy markers with non-default heading content (rc.18 'Fabric Knowledge Base' heading → rc.19 'Fabric Bootstrap' heading)
Mixed state: one file has legacy markers, another has new markers, third has no markers — assert each handled correctly
File without ANY markers (pre-rc.16 state): assert install adds new markers, no migration error

concerns.rationale: All snapshot tests use readFileSync(path, 'utf8') without normalizeLineEndings, and templates ship with LF. On Windows, git's autocrlf may convert checked-out template files to CRLF, causing byte-equality assertions to fail. The codex-mcp-install test uses 'utf8' verbatim and works on POSIX in CI — Windows is the risk surface.
concerns.mitigation: Either (a) normalize line endings before comparison in rc.19 tests (e.g. content.replace(/\r\n/g, '\n')), OR (b) use describe.skipIf(process.platform === 'win32') for byte-equality cases (see install-skills-and-hooks L323 POSIX-only pattern), OR (c) set .gitattributes 'text eol=lf' on the relevant templates. Recommend (a) for assertion robustness.

concerns.concern: Flaky tests from parallel runner timing
concerns.rationale: createWerewolfFixtureRoot includes pid + Date.now() + random suffix so collisions are theoretically impossible. Existing tests are deterministic. The only flake risk is FABRIC_HOME pollution if a test forgets the beforeEach isolation — covered by the server/doctor.test.ts pattern but new rc.19 tests must remember it.
concerns.mitigation: Adopt the FABRIC_HOME isolation block (server/doctor.test.ts L20-L40) verbatim in every rc.19 doctor test that touches personal scope.

concerns.concern: Fixture maintenance — adding rc.19 markers means existing tests break
concerns.rationale: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L442-L443 hardcodes 'fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end' — when rc.19 migrates to 'fabric:bootstrap:*', these constants and the four tests beneath them will fail. This is INTENDED but requires a coordinated update.
concerns.mitigation: Plan a single PR that: (1) updates install code to emit new markers + migration logic, (2) updates the SECTION_BEGIN/SECTION_END constants in install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts, (3) adds new tests in a new file for the migration path. Do NOT split across PRs.

concerns.concern: Hidden coupling between runInit and runInitScan
concerns.rationale: runInit (helpers L99-L112) drives buildInitExecutionPlan + executeInitExecutionPlan with skipMcp:true. There's also a separate runInitScan called by doctor --rescan (see doctor.test.ts L546-L594). If rc.19 changes the install plan stages (e.g. adds a 'snapshot' stage), runInit may need a new flag or the helper itself may need updating.
concerns.mitigation: If rc.19 adds new install stages, prefer extending the existing options shape ({skipMcp, planOnly} → {skipMcp, planOnly, skipSnapshot}) rather than creating a parallel helper. Keep the single source of truth in init-test-utils.ts.

concerns.concern: Mocked-server tests don't catch real server-side regressions
concerns.rationale: packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts mocks @fenglimg/fabric-server entirely. A drift code added on the service side won't be detected by CLI tests unless the mocks are kept in sync. The rc.19 drift detection NEEDS both layers: service-level (real logic) + CLI-level (flag plumbing).
concerns.mitigation: Adopt the two-file convention from rc.15 onwards: service tests at packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts (real runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix); CLI tests at packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts (mocked server, asserts on flag plumbing + exit codes + stdout format). rc.19 drift tests must land in BOTH files.

concerns.concern: Codex CLI nested AGENTS.md is not testable in vitest
concerns.rationale: Codex's discovery of nested AGENTS.md happens at Codex runtime, not within our process. We can only assert on the file STATE we create — we can't observe how Codex consumes it without spawning the Codex binary, which is out of scope for unit/integration tests.
concerns.mitigation: Recommendation: treat nested-AGENTS.md as a docs-only deliverable. Optionally add a thin assertion test on the documentation content (greps for the canonical guidance string) — see rc19_task_to_pattern_map entry for this task.

summary_findings.test_runner_confirmed: vitest 3.2.4 across all packages, invoked via 'vitest run' per package or 'pnpm -r --if-present test' from monorepo root
summary_findings.existing_managed_block_patterns: install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L442-L574 is the canonical reference — 4 tests covering presence, idempotency, in-place replace, vandalism overwrite. rc.19 should mirror this file's shape verbatim for the three-end propagation and marker migration suites. codex-mcp-install.test.ts is the gold standard for legacy→new migration with regex-based occurrence counting.
summary_findings.tmpdir_conventions_documented: Two conventions: (A) createWerewolfFixtureRoot for project-rooted integration tests, (B) raw mkdtempSync for unit-level writer tests. rc.19 uses (A) for all 5 task tests except optionally one (B)-style for a managed-block writer if introduced.
summary_findings.drift_test_approach_proposed: Service-layer byte-equality check producing a 'managed_block_drift' (or 'bootstrap_snapshot_drift') fixable_error code. seedDriftedFile triggers drift; runDoctorReport surfaces it; runDoctorFix re-canonicalizes. CLI-layer test mirrors doctor.test.ts mocked-server pattern.
summary_findings.marker_migration_approach_proposed: Materialize legacy state via writeFixtureFile (no new fixture), run `fab doctor --fix` to trigger migration, assert legacy markers gone + new markers exactly once + surrounding user content intact + idempotent re-runs. Mirror codex-mcp-install.test.ts L146-L171 line-for-line.
summary_findings.key_concerns_flagged: CRLF on Windows (mitigation: normalize line endings in assertions); fixture-maintenance coupling (mitigation: single PR for marker rename); two-layer drift testing (mitigation: write tests in BOTH service and CLI suites).
